mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending forecasts and driving immediate positive equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0 percent over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenue guidance and sustains positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns in more than 40% of rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, an industrial production rebound signals supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost volatility and supports equity multiples over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns show no statistical difference between INDPRO uptrends and downtrends during post-shock periods (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 1-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points signals aggressive policy tightening, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25 percentage points in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following a 0.25pp monthly FEDFUNDS jump over a 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate of 2.0 percent confirms structural economic expansion, which mechanically lifts corporate profit margins and sustains positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC between quarterly GDPC1 growth surprises and 60-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth acceleration and consumer price inflation above 2.5 percent triggers a wage-price spiral expectation, which forces the Federal Reserve to hike rates aggressively and compresses equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of historical instances where both series cross thresholds simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion environments, a peak in industrial production followed by a decline signals capacity constraints and demand exhaustion, which triggers a mean-reversion decline in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO peaks and declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances following a two-month INDPRO decline during late-cycle periods.
